ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-04-2025 Off Calendar

ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-17-2022 Set For Trial On Mar-06-23 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

DEPT 206 ASBESTOS TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-17-2023 Set For Trial On May-20-24 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Substituting Catalina Polson As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substituting Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff; For Leave To File First Amended Complaint; And To Add Previously Sued Doe Defendants To The First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-06-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Sarah Mojarro of MANNING, GROSS & MASSENBURG (949) 892-4700, appearing for defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company.

RULING:
Not contested, not argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Substituting Catalina Polson as Successor-in-Interest to, and Substituting Successor-in-Interest for, Deceased Plaintiff; Granting Leave to File First Amended Complaint; and Granting Leave to Add Previously Sued Doe Defendants to the First Amended Complaint is GRANTED pursuant to C.C.P. sections 377.20, 377.31, 377.60, and 473. No opposition filed. As of the date of the granting of Plaintiff's motion, the First Amended Complaint is deemed served on all Defendants who have previously appeared. Order signed in open court. Judge: Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/SKF)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Dec-29-2022 Vacated

ASBESTOS SETTLEMENT CALENDAR
Off Calendar Dec-29-2022 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Dec-29-2022 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Nov-17-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs Billy Polson and Catalina Polson.
Fred Gatt of LEADER, BERKON, COLAO & SILVERSTEIN LLP (213-234-1750), appearing for defendant Imo Industries, Inc.
Timothy C. Pieper of POND NORTH LLP (213-617-6170), appearing for defendant FMC Corporation.
Gabrielle Consolo of DLA PIPER (US), LLP (310-595-3000), appearing for defendant 3M Company.
Derek S. Johnson of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant General Electric Company and Paramount Global.
Danny S. Kim of CMBG3 LAW LLC (949-467-9500), appearing for defendant Armstrong International, Inc.
Carmen Sandgren of MANNING, GROSS & MASSENBURG (949-892-4700), appearing for defendant Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under Code of Civil Procedure section 36(a). Conditional opposition filed by FMC Corporation. Reply filed by Plaintiffs. The Court did not consider the conditional non-opposition by Goodyear Tire & Rubber for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court. (Local Rule 2.7B.)

The trial date is March 6, 2023 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is March 17, 2023. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is March 2, 2023. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. Fact discovery cut-off date is February 17, 2023. Expert discovery cut-off date is March 3, 2023. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than December 16, 2022. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR ISSUANCE OF TENTATIVE RULING ONLY)
Off Calendar Nov-17-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than December 1, 2022, plaintiff must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. No later than December 1, 2022, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on January 12, 2023 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than January 20, 2023. Responses to the demand are due no later than February 3, 2023. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by February 24, 2023 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, March 2, 2023 at 9 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
